other_observers: None with me, but others were in the area.
specific_location: Manitou Lake and inlet stream on both sides of Hwy 67.
description: Largish Sterna tern whose main diagnostic feature as a long, thin black bill with prominent yellow tip. Otherwise, as with other Sterna terns, body plumage basically white with light gray upperparts and wings. The tern sported a solid black cap that extended from the base of the upper mandible, in line with the eye, to the hindcrown. I observed the bird only in flight, so did not notice whether it had a small crest or not. Secondaries and inner primaries were bright white. Outer primaries were dusky gray with blackish edging to the outermost five primaries (as counted in the occupying photo).Behavior: Observed foraging at beaver pond, plunge diving a couple times otherwise flying with powerful wingstrokes modestly high above the water.Call: None heard.Plumage: Adult breeding
